articles.title: AAP slams Delhi Police for shifting Wangchuk to hospital, using force on protesters
articles.sum: AAP MP Sanjay Singh alleged that the action was taken ahead of the protest's proposed march to Parliament on July 20 and appealed to the youth to continue supporting Mr. Wangchuk's agitation

articles.title: Opposition support grows as Wangchuk’s hunger strike continues on day 20
articles.sum: The protest seeking Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation attracted more high-profile visitors

articles.title: Sonam Wangchuk shifted to Safdarjung Hospital on 21st day of hunger strike
articles.sum: The New Delhi DCP office said that Sonam Wangchuk has been shifted to the hospital as per the orders of the Delhi High Court and on expert medical advice due to his deteriorating health condition

articles.title: India’s first private orbital rocket Vikram-1 set for lift-off
articles.sum: Vikram-1’s flight sequence, from lift-off to orbit injection, will cover 14 phases, and the total duration of the flight will be 15.46 minutes

articles.title: Nothing should be administered to Sonam Wangchuk without my consent, says wife Gitanjali Angmo
articles.sum: The Delhi Police has shifted activist Sonam Wangchuk to the Safdarjung Hospital, his wife Gitanjali J Angmo said on Saturday, July 18

articles.title: IMA plans protest against assault on doctors in Dombivli on July 20
articles.sum: The IMA has urged government to take strict action against all those involved in the assault, without any political interference and to amend the Maharashtra Medicare Act to prevent violence against healthcare workers.

articles.title: The Krishna Janmabhoomi and Shahi Idgah timeline: History, compromise & legal battle
articles.sum: Through the centuries, the Krishna Janmabhoomi temple has been ‘reclaimed’ several times before a compromise agreement was struck with the Shahi Idgah mosque. This is now being challenged.

articles.title: How does a hydrogen train work?
articles.sum: Hydrogen trains are an innovative solution for clean transit, producing zero direct emissions through fuel cells that convert hydrogen into electricity. These trains can seamlessly run on existing railway lines without needing overhead power lines. India’s version operates with c...

articles.title: 'Historic new frontier': PM Modi hails 'talent of youth' ahead of Skyroot's maiden orbital attempt with Vikram-1 launch
articles.sum: Skyroot Aerospace prepares for the maiden orbital launch of Vikram-1 today. Prime Minister Modi hailed this as a historic new frontier for India. This mission marks the first time a private Indian company attempts an orbital launch. Vikram-1 is a four-stage rocket designed for ra...

articles.title: 'There was no need to bring him to hospital', says Sonam Wangchuk's wife; CJP attacks Modi govt - top developments
articles.sum: Activist Sonam Wangchuk was forcibly removed from Jantar Mantar and subsequently taken to a hospital as his health declined after twenty-one days of an indefinite hunger strike. During the demonstration, police detained CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke amid the ensuing chaos. Despite W...

articles.title: When the classroom teaches children how to think
articles.sum: Times Critical Thinking Championship visited a school where students engaged in debate and real-life scenarios to discover how facts and reasoning enable better decision-making

articles.title: Delhi high court order has crippled consular, visa services at 4 missions: Govt to Supreme Court
articles.sum: Visa and consular services are severely impacted in four Indian missions abroad. The Delhi High Court had quashed the selection of private service providers for these crucial functions. The Centre informed the Supreme Court that these services are now at a standstill. This cancel...

articles.title: House panel pumps brakes on report in middle of voting
articles.sum: Defers adoption of report on Contentious bill to oust PM, CMs

articles.title: 1 in 4 Kerala candidates skips NEET, Rajasthan posts 69% success
articles.sum: NEET-UG 2026 attendance revealed regional disparities across India. Kerala, Tamil Nadu, and Karnataka experienced significant candidate absenteeism this year. Rajasthan, however, saw increased registrations and a strong qualification rate. Other northern states like Uttar Pradesh...

articles.title: Diamonds and 'cab to orbit': The rocket aiming to transform India's space ambitions
articles.sum: India's first space-tech unicorn Skyroot Aerospace is set to launch Vikram-1 on Saturday.

articles.title: WCA concerned about ODI World Cup format
articles.sum: The World Cricketers' Association says it is "concerned" by the changes made to the structure of the men's 50-over World Cup.

articles.title: Crackdown on 'objectionable' books in Indian-administered Kashmir sparks row
articles.sum: Authorities in Indian-administered Kashmir have asked educational institutions to review every book on their premises.

articles.title: Air India crash report likely to be ready in October, say investigators
articles.sum: The crash killed 260 people, including 241 on board, with only one passenger surviving.

articles.sum: The free trade agreement between the world's fifth and sixth largest economies has come into effect - can it deliver?

articles.title: How India's Lord's win echoes the legacy of its pioneering women cricketers
articles.sum: India won the first women's Test at Lord's, 142 years after its first men's Test.
